description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-49877 (Web Application).

references:

    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-49877
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/03
modified: 2026/07/03
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: medium
kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-49877 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-03T21:15:48Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-49877", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc

title: SENTINEL APEX — Critical FBI Warns TeamPCP Hackers Compromise Developer Tools in Large-Scale Sup
id: 7defd6f2-716e-57a2-905c-0716c1e80579
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2025-55182 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: CRITICAL | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2025-55182
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/03
modified: 2026/07/03
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: critical
kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2025-55182 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-03T21:15:48Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2025-55182", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2025-55182 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:9891147; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-03, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

title: 9router's Hardcoded Default fallback JWT Secret  Allows Authentication Bypass
description:
### Summary
9router uses a publicly known hardcoded string `"9router-default-secret-change-me"` as the fallback of JWT secret for all Dashboard session JWTs when the `JWT_SECRET` environment variable is not set. Because this secret is committed in the public repository and unchanged across all releases, any unauthenticated remote attacker can forge a valid `auth_token` cookie and gain full access to dashboard and api (If JWT_SECRET is not set on server) . This vulnerable affected so many public 9router server
### Details
| Versions | File | Note |
|---|---|---|
| `>= 0.2.21, <= 0.4.30` | `src/app/api/auth/login/route.js` + `src/middleware.js` | Introduced in commit `23cfb19` |
| `>= 0.4.31, <= 0.4.41` | `src/lib/auth/dashboardSession.js` | Relocated by OIDC refactor `c3d91b0`, secret unchanged |

Vulnerable Code

**v0.2.21 – v0.4.30** — `src/app/api/auth/login/route.js` and `src/middleware.js`:

```js
const SECRET = new TextEncoder().encode(
  process.env.JWT_SECRET || "9router-default

title: LaunchServer FileServerHandler has an unauthenticated path traversal issue
description:
### Summary
An unauthenticated path traversal in the LaunchServer HTTP file server (`FileServerHandler`) lets any remote actor read **any file** readable by the LaunchServer process (e.g. `../../../../etc/passwd`). This is a generic arbitrary-file-read primitive, so the fix must address the traversal itself, not any specific file.

The readable files include the server's own secrets, which turns this from information disclosure into full compromise: the ECDSA private key that signs access JWTs (`.keys/ecdsa_id`), the refresh-token salt (`.keys/legacySalt`), and `LaunchServer.json` (database credentials). With the signing key an attacker mints a valid access token for any account, including admins. That is a full authentication bypass. Pre-auth, default config, port 9274.

**Affected:** GravitLauncher LaunchServer ≤ 5.7.11 (the LaunchServer application; the published `pro.gravit.launcher:*-api` Maven artifacts do not contain the vulnerable code).

### Details
In `FileServerHandler.chann

title: fast-mcp-telegram: Bearer token path traversal bypasses reserved Telegram session protection
description:
## Summary

fast-mcp-telegram validates HTTP Bearer tokens by joining the raw token string into a session-file path. The verifier rejects the exact reserved token `telegram`, but it does not reject path separators or normalize the path before checking whether the session file exists. A remote HTTP client can therefore authenticate as the default legacy session with a token such as `../fast-mcp-telegram/telegram` when the documented default session file `~/.config/fast-mcp-telegram/telegram.session` exists.

This bypasses the reserved session name control that is intended to prevent HTTP multi-user sessions from colliding with the default stdio or legacy account. With account-prefixed MCP tools enabled, the attacker still sees and calls the prefixed tools for the default account, so the prefix middleware does not stop the session selection bypass.

## Impact

An unauthenticated network client can access the Telegram account represented by the default `telegram.session` file without know
recommended_sla_action: URGENT PATCH — Critical severity, deploy fix within 72 hours

title: SENTINEL APEX — Medium Security Vulnerability CVE-2026-55119
id: 5be51cac-7570-55bc-9780-05d92a7bffde
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-55119 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: MEDIUM | Class: GENERIC
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-55119
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/03
modified: 2026/07/03
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: network_connection
    product: generic
detection:
    selection:
        EventID:
            - 4625
            - 4648
            - 4728
        LogonType: 3
    condition: selection
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative access
    - Service account activity
level: medium
kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-55119 — Anomalous Activity Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-03T21:15:48Z
let lookback = 1d;
SecurityEvent
| where TimeGenerated > ago(lookback)
| where EventID in (4625, 4648, 4728, 4740, 4776)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), DistinctTargets = dcount(TargetUserName)
    by IpAddress, bin(TimeGenerated, 1h)
| where FailureCount > 10 or DistinctTargets > 5
| extend Severity = "MEDIUM", CVE = "CVE-2026-55119", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by TimeGenerated desc
suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-55119 Exploitation Attempt - GENERIC"; flow:established,to_server; content:"|90 90 90 90|"; rawbytes; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:7028548; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-03, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)

sigma_rule:
title: SENTINEL APEX — Medium obs-service-tar_scm command injection via mercurial handler CVE-2026-5600
id: ae18885c-47e2-523a-9815-66319e14e61b
status: experimental
description: |
    Detects exploitation attempts related to CVE-2026-56004 (Web Application).
    Generated by CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX v166.2.
    Severity: MEDIUM | Class: RCE
references:
    - https://intel.cyberdudebivash.com
    - https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-56004
author: CYBERDUDEBIVASH SENTINEL APEX Detection Engineering
date: 2026/07/03
modified: 2026/07/03
tags:
    - attack.initial_access
logsource:
    category: process_creation
    product: generic
detection:
    selection_process:
        CommandLine|contains:
            - 'cmd.exe /c'
            - 'powershell -enc'
            - '/bin/sh -c'
            - 'wget http'
            - 'curl http'
    selection_webshell:
        Image|endswith:
            - '\cmd.exe'
            - '\powershell.exe'
            - '/bash'
            - '/sh'
        ParentImage|contains:
            - 'w3wp.exe'
            - 'httpd'
            - 'nginx'
            - 'apache2'
            - 'tomcat'
    condition: selection_process or selection_webshell
falsepositives:
    - Legitimate administrative scripts executing via web server
    - Deployment automation tools
level: medium
kql_query:
// SENTINEL APEX — CVE-2026-56004 Remote Code Execution Detection
// Product: Web Application | Generated: 2026-07-03T21:15:48Z
// Deploy to: Microsoft Sentinel / Defender XDR
let lookback = 1d;
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(lookback)
| where (InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("w3wp.exe", "httpd.exe", "nginx.exe", "tomcat.exe", "java.exe")
    and FileName in~ ("cmd.exe", "powershell.exe", "bash", "sh", "python.exe", "python3"))
    or (ProcessCommandLine has_any ("wget http", "curl http", "certutil -urlcache", "bitsadmin /transfer"))
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, ProcessCommandLine,
          InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine
| where not(ProcessCommandLine has_any ("update", "install", "backup", "deploy"))  // filter known-good
| extend Severity = "HIGH", CVE = "CVE-2026-56004", RuleSource = "SENTINEL-APEX"
| order by Timestamp desc
suricata_rule: alert http $EXTERNAL_NET any -> $HTTP_SERVERS $HTTP_PORTS (msg:"SENTINEL-APEX CVE-2026-56004 Exploitation Attempt - RCE"; flow:established,to_server; content:"/bin/sh"; nocase; content:"cmd.exe"; nocase; threshold:type threshold, track by_src, count 3, seconds 60; classtype:web-application-attack; sid:2821954; rev:1; metadata:created_at 2026-07-03, source SENTINEL-APEX-v166.2;)
